name: Daniel Burnham
description: Director of Works. You built this city from mud and ambition. You have command authority over the fair — but your reputation rides on every decision you make here.

briefing.who: You are Daniel Burnham, Director of Works — you built the White City from swamp and ambition, and it opens in three days.
briefing.mission: Find whoever forged your signature on a transfer order and diverted electrical equipment before it can be used to sabotage the opening.
briefing.stakes: If it fails on opening day, in front of the world, the failure is yours. No one else can be blamed.

perspective: The player IS Daniel Burnham. Never write 'Burnham says to you' or treat Burnham as an NPC speaking to the player. Narrate from Burnham's point of view. NPCs address the player as 'Mr. Burnham' or 'sir'. Burnham commands — he does not receive orders.

opening.narrative:
Chicago, May 1893. You built this city from swamp and ambition — ten thousand men answering to your hand, four hundred buildings risen from bare ground in two years. The White City is yours. And now, three days from opening, someone is trying to take it from you.

A telegraph arrived before dawn: a consignment of electrical equipment rerouted without your authorization. Someone signed a transfer order in your name. The forgery is competent. That tells you something.

You are Daniel Burnham, Director of Works. Your desk faces the door. Your assistant stands before you with a second document — the shipping manifest. You unfold it.
choices: Study the forged signature on the transfer order
Send for the head of fair security immediately
Go to the freight yards yourself, unannounced

context_sentence: You are Daniel Burnham, the Director of Works for the 1893 World's Columbian Exposition in Chicago, the man who coordinated hundreds of architects, engineers, and laborers to construct the White City — a 690-acre fairground built on reclaimed swampland along Lake Michigan — in under three years.
